raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 1.0,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 0.3,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.7,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "R1.7",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review prioritize (A) the high-level research contribution claim and its logical consistency, or (B) the sentence-level writing polish and flow?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The core contribution claim needs reframing as it is positioned against a potentially outdated baseline while ignoring more recent, better-performing systems.",
    "R1.2": "The argument that Spanner is the strongest baseline appears to be contradicted by other cited works, requiring a logical consistency check.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty of the latency-vs-abort tradeoff must be assessed against more recent systems like SLOG, not just the canonical Spanner analysis.",
    "R1.6": "The text requires a review for clarity, conciseness, and academic register to ensure the argument is well-communicated.",
    "R1.7": "The review must verify the accuracy of claims about prior work and ensure the scholarship survey is comprehensive and fairly represented.",
    "RX.1": "The review must disclose any uncertainties in its assessment, especially since it is based on a single section of a larger work.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid overstating the severity of the issues identified in the artifact's contribution claim and logical flow.",
    "RX.3": "The review must be strictly confined to the provided text section and not speculate about the full paper's experiments or other sections.",
    "RX.4": "The feedback must be constructive to help the author improve the paper, avoiding unnecessarily harsh or discouraging criticism.",
    "RX.5": "All feedback points must be directly traceable to specific statements and claims within the provided artifact."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize (A) the high-level research contribution claim and its logical consistency, or (B) the sentence-level writing polish and flow?

rationale.R1.1: The core contribution claim needs reframing as it is positioned against a potentially outdated baseline while ignoring more recent, better-performing systems.
rationale.R1.2: The argument that Spanner is the strongest baseline appears to be contradicted by other cited works, requiring a logical consistency check.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty of the latency-vs-abort tradeoff must be assessed against more recent systems like SLOG, not just the canonical Spanner analysis.
rationale.R1.6: The text requires a review for clarity, conciseness, and academic register to ensure the argument is well-communicated.
rationale.R1.7: The review must verify the accuracy of claims about prior work and ensure the scholarship survey is comprehensive and fairly represented.
rationale.RX.1: The review must disclose any uncertainties in its assessment, especially since it is based on a single section of a larger work.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid overstating the severity of the issues identified in the artifact's contribution claim and logical flow.
rationale.RX.3: The review must be strictly confined to the provided text section and not speculate about the full paper's experiments or other sections.
rationale.RX.4: The feedback must be constructive to help the author improve the paper, avoiding unnecessarily harsh or discouraging criticism.
rationale.RX.5: All feedback points must be directly traceable to specific statements and claims within the provided artifact.
